data_sources: Granola (queried — partial results; Slack used as primary due to Granola data conflicts)

excluded_deals.name: Remix Medical
excluded_deals.reason: Closed out 4/7 — no response since Feb 25 across multiple follow-ups; contact was not the decision maker

excluded_deals.name: Hemorrhoid Centers of America
excluded_deals.reason: Deal broken off by Solv 3/31 — Dr. Chrono EHR incompatibility; no viable integration path

deals.name: CLS Health
deals.size: $3,500/mo (up to 3,000 telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/visit ClearPay; 250 providers total; Texas; 15–30 providers and 15–30 telemed visits/day to start
deals.champion: Abigail (EMR/technical lead); Dr. Attia (clinical champion); CLS President (executive sponsor, actively applying pressure to move)
deals.status_narrative: CLS came back on 4/24 with three pricing requests framed as 'help us make it easier to sign with Solv': (1) shift from flat monthly fee to per-encounter or hybrid pricing, (2) shorten initial term from 1 year to 6 months, (3) reduce or waive the $2K implementation fee. They also opened a design partner conversation on ClearPay for specialty clinics (auth/referral workflows) and confirmed Luma cannot do dynamic eligibility. Critical open question: whether ECW supports SolvPay — this gates the deal scope. President is engaged and applying pressure on Abigail's team. June 1 target launch. Using Commure for AI scribe.
key_risks: ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — gating the full deal scope
Pricing concession requests (per-encounter model, 6-month term, waived impl fee) may require finance approval and compress margin
EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year — prospect wants short-term contract flexibility
deals.next_step: Confirm ECW + SolvPay feasibility (Owner: Teresa/Brantley, ASAP). Evaluate pricing flexibility on hybrid model, 6-month term, and implementation fee (Owner: Katie/Teresa, this week). Respond to CLS before president meeting.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Pricing objections received 4/24; ECW+SolvPay compatibility is the critical blocker to close.

deals.name: GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)
deals.size: Free during pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay usage at scale; 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs post-acquisition; ~2K calls/week; starting with 2 locations
deals.champion: Jim (CEO), engaged but skeptical; Deana (Clinical Manager), skeptical of AI scheduling complexity
deals.status_narrative: Contract is being actively drafted as of 4/24 (Katie and Maria). Pricing locked: free during pilot, then $1.25/resolved call and $0.40/ClearPay at scale. Jim said on the 4/21 pricing call that he needs to understand eventual pricing and ROI before committing time to the pilot. Org recently 3x'd in size via two acquisitions (14 → 40 physicians, 20 → 100 PAs). No ortho reference customer remains the hardest objection throughout all conversations; Jim softened on this over the 4/2 call but it remains a real risk.
key_risks: No ortho reference customer — Jim's stated primary objection; won't move without a peer reference
ROI framing is weak in low-competition market — Jim pushes back that missed calls don't equal missed patients
Collections enforcement requires leadership culture change at GLOC (Jim acknowledged this explicitly)
deals.next_step: Finalize and send contract (Owner: Katie/Maria, in progress as of 4/24).
deals.key_notes_one_line: Contract in draft as of 4/24; no ortho reference customer is the persistent risk to close.

deals.name: Plymouth Bay Orthopedic Associates
deals.size: 1 location (standalone ortho urgent care, opening June 1); 3 additional ortho clinics as expansion potential; MRR TBD; ECW
deals.locations: 1 active (urgent care focus); 3 additional ortho clinics in scope for expansion
deals.champion: Christopher LaFave (Executive Director, 1yr tenure, decision authority); Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, 4mo tenure, ex-PhysicianOne, former Solv user — internally tasked to evaluate Solv)
deals.status_narrative: Strong 4/8 demo with four stakeholders (Executive Director, Ops Manager, Clinical Manager, Dir. of Marketing). No major UX or workflow objections; walk-ins, same-day booking, and queue management all landed well for the urgent care use case. Verbal interest confirmed; team doing internal review and expected to reconnect in ~1 week from 4/12. June 1 opening is a real forcing function. ECW integration confidence is the primary gate — Christopher explicitly pushed for a demo showing data actually flowing into ECW. NOTE: Granola showed this as a no-go/punted to standard sales; this directly conflicts with active Slack data through 4/12. Trusting Slack.
key_risks: ECW live integration demo is a hard gating factor — Christopher needs to see data flow, not just hear about it
Referral workflow gap blocks expansion beyond urgent care to the other 3 ortho clinics (HMO referral requirement)
June 1 timeline may be too tight for clean implementation if decision delays
deals.next_step: Brantley to send deck + ECW integration packet (was due Mon/Tues after 4/8 — likely overdue). Plymouth Bay team to complete internal review and schedule reconnect call.
deals.next_step_timing: Reconnect expected week of 4/14–4/18 (likely past due — follow-up needed)
deals.granola_conflict_note: Granola returned stale data showing this deal as no-go/punted to standard sales. Slack data from 4/12 shows an active deal with strong engagement. Slack used as authoritative.
deals.key_notes_one_line: 4-stakeholder demo landed 4/8; ECW live integration proof is the gate; reconnect likely overdue.

deals.name: Premier Ortho
deals.size: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; Athena; MRR TBD
deals.champion: Jon Borja (COO), self-advocating and transparent — proactively communicated the Assort blocker
deals.status_narrative: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon (COO) proactively communicated that contractual obligations to Assort Health block all movement — 'our hands are tied at the moment' — with no timeline for resolution given. This deal was previously at near-Paper Process stage (DocuSign was ready) before the Assort blocker surfaced. Jon confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear. Brantley checking in passively every ~2 weeks. No new update since 3/30 outreach. Next check-in was targeted for ~4/14 and may be overdue.
key_risks: Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal fully stalled with no active path forward
Prolonged pause erodes urgency and creates competitive exposure during the wait window
No active outreach risk — if Assort resolves and another vendor moves in first, deal is lost
deals.next_step: Passive monitoring only. Jon holds the ball. Brantley next periodic check-in (targeted ~4/14, likely overdue — check in now).
deals.key_notes_one_line: Assort contract is a hard block with no timeline; Jon intends to return when clear.

deals.name: Total Primary Care
deals.size: 47 locations confirmed; ECW with CTO-built homegrown scheduler (will not be replaced); acquisition-layer scope only; MRR TBD
deals.champion: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
deals.status_narrative: Effectively paused/cut off as of 4/7. TPC's CTO-built scheduler is locked in — 'too many resources poured into it' — and they will not replace it. Rikin wants Solv as an acquisition layer only (GMB routing to Solv booking) running in parallel with their existing scheduler. Not on their timeline for 6-9 months. Also actively piloting a competing voice agent they declined to name. Team was told to consider shutting down or parking. This is a 'Primary Care Connect' type deal structure, not a standard Monarch fit.
key_risks: 6-9 month self-stated timeline with zero commitment — long, low-probability hold
Acquisition-layer-only scope is non-standard — Solv product team appetite for this model is unknown
Competing voice agent pilot already underway with Rikin's team
deals.next_step: Team decision required: park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or close out. If pursuing, validate acquisition-layer + ECW integration feasibility first (Owner: Kapeesh/Teresa). Decision needed now.
deals.next_step_timing: Decision needed now; re-engage Q3 2026 if parked
deals.key_notes_one_line: Paused 6-9 months; acquisition-only scope; team needs to decide to park or close.

deals.name: Elevate Health Group
deals.deal_type: Expansion (inferred — Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care)
deals.size: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; Athena; SoCal; ClearPay only at ~$0.50/visit
deals.champion: Katia Sarafian (Administrator, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner, explicit objection)
deals.status_narrative: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian (owner) pushed back explicitly on 3/16: 'This doesn't make sense for our practice. 90% of our patients are recurring and their insurances are stable.' ClearPay's point-of-care estimate model doesn't land for a high-recurring primary care patient mix. No response to follow-up requests for a leadership call since 3/24. Real collection pain exists (post-visit statements, IPA routing errors) but the product-fit gap is genuine. Team is not pushing hard given Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care.
key_risks: Product-fit mismatch is explicit — owner's objection is substantive, not just pricing or timing
Dark since mid-March — effectively ghosting on all follow-ups
Aggressive pursuit risks damaging the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership
deals.next_step: One final low-touch follow-up only. Do not push. Re-engage if ClearPay model evolves to better fit recurring primary care. Owner: Brantley. No active pursuit.
deals.next_step_timing: No active pursuit; potential re-engagement if ClearPay model evolves
deals.key_notes_one_line: Owner's explicit product-fit objection; near dead; don't push — Mederi partnership at risk.

early_stage_watch.name: OS1 Orthopedic & Sports Injury Clinics
early_stage_watch.contact: Randy Johansen (board member, former CEO of AFC)
early_stage_watch.notes: Intro via Heather at UCA happy hour. First substantive call 4/24 with Brantley. 4 locations on ECW, cash-constrained (opened 3 locs at once; Atlanta/Kennesaw location struggling badly without MRI for months). Randy meeting with OS1 team on Wednesday (4/22) to assess appetite for reviewing solutions. Interested in Maya and ClearPay materials. Reconnecting late April/early May. Evan Cory to help prepare materials. Explicit flag: they are cash-strapped.

executive_summaries.cls_health:
**CLS Health** — Paper Process | New Logo
- **Size**: $3,500/mo (up to 3,000 telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/ClearPay; 250 providers total; 15–30 providers and visits/day to start
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Abigail (EMR/technical lead); Dr. Attia (clinical); CLS President actively engaged and applying pressure
- **Status**: CLS came back 4/24 with three pricing requests framed as wanting to sign: shift to per-encounter/hybrid pricing, shorten to 6-month first term, and waive the $2K implementation fee. ECW+SolvPay compatibility remains unconfirmed and gates the full deal scope. Luma confirmed they cannot do dynamic eligibility — Solv's key edge. June 1 target launch date.
- **Key risks**:
  - ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — this gates the entire deal scope
  - Hybrid pricing model and 6-month term need Solv finance sign-off before close
  - EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year; CLS wants contract flexibility
- **Next step**: Confirm ECW + SolvPay feasibility (Owner: Teresa/Brantley, ASAP). Respond to pricing requests (Owner: Katie/Teresa, this week).
executive_summaries.gloc:
**GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)** — Paper Process | New Logo (Design Partner)
- **Size**: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale; 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs; ~2K calls/week; starting with 2 locations
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jim (CEO), engaged but skeptical; Deana (Clinical Manager), skeptical of AI scheduling complexity
- **Status**: Contract is being drafted as of 4/24 (Katie + Maria). Pricing locked at free pilot then usage-based at scale. Jim conditioned pilot participation on understanding eventual pricing and ROI to present to his partners. No ortho reference customer remains the hardest objection throughout all conversations.
- **Key risks**:
  - No ortho reference customer — Jim's stated primary objection; peer reference is a hard ask
  - ROI framing is weak in low-competition market (missed calls ≠ missed patients per Jim)
  - Collections enforcement requires leadership culture change that Jim acknowledged they must make
- **Next step**: Finalize and send contract. Owner: Katie/Maria. In progress as of 4/24.
executive_summaries.plymouth_bay_ortho:
**Plymouth Bay Orthopedic Associates** — Building Our Champion | New Logo
- **Size**: 1 location (ortho urgent care, June 1 opening); 3 additional ortho clinics as expansion potential; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Christopher LaFave (Executive Director, decision authority); Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, former Solv user, internally tasked to evaluate Solv)
- **Status**: Strong 4/8 demo with 4 stakeholders — no major objections, urgent care use case is a clean fit. Team doing internal review with reconnect expected ~1 week from 4/12 (likely overdue). ECW live integration demo is the primary gate — Christopher wants to see data actually flowing into ECW. NOTE: Granola shows this as a no-go; this conflicts with Slack data from 4/12. Trusting Slack.
- **Key risks**:
  - ECW integration confidence is gate — they consistently find ECW tech integrations difficult
  - Referral workflow gap blocks expansion to the 3 other ortho clinics
  - Reconnect window may be slipping past the forcing function of June 1 opening
- **Next step**: Brantley to send ECW integration packet (may be overdue from 4/8). Pursue reconnect call. Owner: Brantley. Due: ASAP.
executive_summaries.premier_ortho:
**Premier Ortho** — Prospecting (Paused — was near Paper Process) | New Logo
- **Size**: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and self-advocating but contractually blocked
- **Status**: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon proactively communicated that Assort Health contractual obligations block all movement — no timeline. Was at near-Paper Process stage before this. Jon confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear. Brantley monitoring passively; check-in targeted for ~4/14, likely overdue.
- **Key risks**:
  - Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal fully stalled with no active path
  - Prolonged pause erodes urgency; competitive risk if another vendor moves during the window
  - No active outreach means deal is Jon's to restart — could go silent
- **Next step**: Brantley to check in now (targeted 4/14 check-in is overdue). Passive monitoring only. Owner: Brantley.
executive_summaries.total_primary_care:
**Total Primary Care** — Prospecting (Paused) | New Logo
- **Size**: 47 locations; ECW; CTO-built scheduler will not be replaced; acquisition-layer scope only; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
- **Status**: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. TPC's homegrown scheduler is locked in. Rikin wants Solv as patient acquisition layer only (GMB → Solv booking), running in parallel with their existing scheduler. Not on timeline for 6-9 months. Actively piloting a competing voice agent. Team was offered the option to shut down. Non-standard Monarch fit.
- **Key risks**:
  - 6-9 month timeline with zero commitment is a long, low-probability hold
  - Acquisition-layer-only scope is non-standard — internal product team appetite unknown
  - Competing voice agent pilot already underway with Rikin's team
- **Next step**: Team decision required — park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or close out. Owner: Brantley/Teresa. Due: now.
executive_summaries.elevate_health_group:
**Elevate Health Group** — Prospecting (Near Dead) | Expansion
- **Size**: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; Athena; SoCal; ClearPay only at ~$0.50/visit
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Katia Sarafian (Administrator, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit product-fit objection
- **Status**: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian pushed back explicitly on 3/16 — '90% of patients are recurring, insurances are stable, doesn't make sense.' ClearPay's workflow doesn't fit recurring primary care. No response to follow-ups since mid-March. Real collection pain exists but the product-fit gap is genuine and owner-level. Pushing hard risks the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership.
- **Key risks**:
  - Product-fit objection is substantive and owner-level — not a pricing or timing issue
  - Dark since mid-March; effectively ghosting all follow-ups
  - Aggressive pursuit risks straining the Mederi Urgent Care existing partnership
- **Next step**: One final low-touch follow-up only. Do not push. Re-engage if ClearPay model evolves for recurring primary care. Owner: Brantley. No active pursuit.

leadership_table.key_notes: Pricing objections 4/24; ECW+SolvPay compatibility is the critical blocker to close.
Contract in draft 4/24; no ortho reference customer is the persistent risk.
ECW live integration demo is a hard gate; reconnect call likely overdue from 4/12.
Assort contract hard block; Jon intends to return when clear — no timeline given.
Paused 6-9 months; non-standard scope; team needs to decide to park or close.
Owner's explicit product-fit objection; don't push — Mederi partnership at risk.
